1st Year.name: General Human Anatomy (including Embryology & Histology)
1st Year.outcomes: Students will describe the gross anatomical structure of the human body with special emphasis on head and neck region.

1st Year.name: General Human Physiology & Biochemistry
1st Year.outcomes: Students will explain the normal functioning of the human body and apply biochemical principles to dental practice.

1st Year.name: Dental Anatomy, Embryology & Oral Histology
1st Year.outcomes: Students will identify, draw, and carve model teeth of all types and describe oral embryology.

2nd Year.name: General & Dental Pharmacology & Therapeutics
2nd Year.outcomes: Students will prescribe appropriate drugs for common dental conditions safely.

2nd Year.name: Dental Materials
2nd Year.outcomes: Students will select and manipulate dental materials correctly for clinical situations.

2nd Year.name: General Pathology & Microbiology
2nd Year.outcomes: Students will explain the pathological basis of disease and apply infection control principles.

2nd Year.name: Pre-Clinical Conservative Dentistry
2nd Year.outcomes: Students will prepare standard cavities in all classes on phantom heads with confidence.

2nd Year.name: Pre-Clinical Prosthodontics
2nd Year.outcomes: Students will fabricate a complete denture on articulators as per class I jaw relationship.

3rd Year.name: Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery I
3rd Year.outcomes: Students will perform forceps and elevator extractions under faculty supervision.

3rd Year.name: Conservative Dentistry & Endodontics I
3rd Year.outcomes: Students will perform Class I–V cavity preparations and begin root canal procedures.

3rd Year.name: Orthodontics & Dentofacial Orthopedics I
3rd Year.outcomes: Students will read and trace cephalograms and fabricate simple removable orthodontic appliances.

3rd Year.name: Oral Pathology & Microbiology
3rd Year.outcomes: Students will diagnose common oral pathological conditions from clinical and histopathological findings.

4th Year.name: Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery II
4th Year.outcomes: Students will assist in and independently perform minor oral surgical procedures.

4th Year.name: Prosthodontics & Crown & Bridge
4th Year.outcomes: Students will fabricate complete and partial dentures and basic fixed prostheses.

4th Year.name: Paediatric & Preventive Dentistry
4th Year.outcomes: Students will manage paediatric patients and perform preventive and basic restorative procedures.

4th Year.name: Periodontology
4th Year.outcomes: Students will perform scaling, root planing, and basic periodontal surgeries.

4th Year.name: Oral Medicine & Radiology
4th Year.outcomes: Students will interpret dental radiographs and diagnose common oral medicine conditions.

4th Year.name: Public Health Dentistry
4th Year.outcomes: Students will conduct oral health surveys and design community dental programs.

Oral Surgery.name: Advanced Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery
Oral Surgery.outcomes: PG students will independently perform complex surgical procedures under supervision.

Conservative & Endodontics.name: Advanced Conservative & Endodontics
Conservative & Endodontics.outcomes: PG students will master advanced root canal and aesthetic restorative procedures.

Orthodontics.name: Advanced Orthodontics
Orthodontics.outcomes: PG students will manage complex malocclusion cases with fixed and functional appliances.

Periodontology.name: Advanced Periodontology
Periodontology.outcomes: PG students will manage advanced periodontal cases and perform regenerative procedures.

Implantology.name: Certificate in Oral Implantology (6 months)
Implantology.outcomes: Participants will perform basic implant placements under supervision with real-time CBCT guidance.

Clear Aligners.name: Certificate in Clear Aligner Therapy (3 months)
Clear Aligners.outcomes: Participants will plan and manage straightforward aligner cases independently.

Laser Dentistry.name: Laser Dentistry — Hands-on Program (2 days)
Laser Dentistry.outcomes: Participants will safely operate dental laser units for soft tissue and endodontic applications.
